what_it_does: An acceptance test script for runtime/cc_channels.py that exercises the cross-session channel router using fake registration files and local throwaway HTTP servers instead of a real Claude session.
observations: Acceptance-style harness with inline PASS/FAIL assertions rather than unittest/pytest classes
Mutates cc module globals (CHAN_DIR, MAIL_LOG, THREADS, CHANNELS_DIR) to redirect state into a temp directory
Uses two local HTTP mocks: _Rx as a channel port and _MockSupervisor as a session supervisor
Tests presence pruning for both port-based channel members and supervised members
Tests fail-loud find resolution, duplicate members, archived channels, and unreachable ports
Covers named-channel registry create/list/add/remove/archive with multi-channel membership
Adds unified transport dispatch: channel (HTTP POST) vs supervised (supervisor /inject + /watch reply fold)
Includes regression tests for stale replay mis-routing and watcher restart after exit
standouts: Faithful mock supervisor that replays prior event history then streams live events, enabling stale-replay guard tests
Stale-own-nonce and stale-foreign-replay guards tested through controlled supervisor history
Watcher lifecycle regression test that forces a /watch stream blip and verifies a fresh watcher folds the next reply
Mixed broadcast across channel and supervised members in a single acceptance flow
conventions: Top-level procedural test script with helper functions check and raises
Global module state mutation to inject temp directories and mock servers
Daemon HTTP server threads for the mock channel port and supervisor
Polling with time.sleep loops to wait for asynchronous HTTP delivery
concerns: Tests are timing-dependent and may flake under slow or loaded runners
Mutates global module state, so test cases are not fully isolated from each other
Uses internal module details such as cc._transport_of, cc._watchers, and cc.CHANNEL_FABRIC_SOURCE
Unreachable supervisor test uses port 9, which assumes network discard behavior
No explicit cleanup of the temp directory on failure or early exit
Long single file mixes many subsystem tests, making failures harder to localize
notes: A broad end-to-end harness that proves the channel router's presence, resolution, mail/threading, reply push-back, named-channel registry, and dual transport dispatch with mocked HTTP backends.
questions: What exact values do cc.CHANNEL_FABRIC_SOURCE and cc.DEFAULT_SUPERVISOR_BASE hold?
How does cc.live_sessions distinguish a transient unreachable supervisor from a closed session?
What is the full production supervisor event schema beyond the mocked injected/done/closed types?
How are thread IDs and nonces generated and matched during supervised reply folding?
What happens if a handle is registered as both a channel member and a supervised member?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the docstring states it tests presence pruning, find resolution, threading, mail record, reply routing, named channels, and unified transport, and the file does exactly that.
apparent_themes: Regression testing for supervised watcher lifecycle
intention_signals: The author wants a single runnable file that proves the whole channel layer works end-to-end without a live Claude, with special emphasis on catching regressions in supervised reply routing and stale event handling.
novelty: A faithful mock supervisor that replays history to test stale-event guards, combined with channel+supervised broadcast acceptance in one script.
contribution: A runnable acceptance contract that demonstrates and guards the channel router's presence, find, mail/thread, reply push-back, named-channel registry, and dual-transport dispatch semantics, including supervised reply-fold edge cases.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test script for runtime/cc_channels.py that exercises presence pruning, fail-loud session lookup, thread/mail logging, reply routing, named-channel registry, and unified channel/supervised transport dispatch using fake registrations and local HTTP mocks.
intention_for_embedding: To provide an end-to-end regression harness for the cross-session channel router without requiring a live Claude session.

symbols.does: Inline assertion helper that records PASS/FAIL and prints the result.
symbols.description: Takes a test name, a boolean condition, and an optional detail string; appends the name to PASS or FAIL and prints a formatted line.

symbols.does: Helper that checks whether a callable raises cc.ChannelError.
symbols.description: Runs the provided function and returns True only if it raises a cc.ChannelError, optionally requiring the error message to contain a substring.

symbols.does: Stand-in HTTP handler representing a channel port that records POSTed JSON bodies.
symbols.description: A BaseHTTPRequestHandler subclass used as the target for channel-member HTTP pushes; it stores every received JSON payload in a class-level list and responds with HTTP 200.

symbols.does: Suppresses default HTTP request logging for the mock channel port.
symbols.description: Overrides BaseHTTPRequestHandler.log_message to do nothing, keeping test output clean.

symbols.does: Reads and records the JSON body of each POST request to the mock channel port.
symbols.description: Parses the Content-Length header, reads the request body, appends the parsed JSON to _Rx.received, and returns a 200 OK response.

symbols.does: Writes a fake port-based channel registration file into the temp directory.
symbols.description: Creates a JSON registration file with handle, cwd, pid, port, description, and other fields so cc.live_sessions and cc.find can resolve it.

symbols.does: Faithful mock of a session supervisor exposing /sessions, /watch, and /inject endpoints.
symbols.description: Maintains per-session state, replay history, live event queues, and inject records; it mirrors the real supervisor's behavior of replaying prior events on /watch connect and streaming live events afterward.

symbols.does: Suppresses default HTTP request logging for the mock supervisor.
symbols.description: Overrides BaseHTTPRequestHandler.log_message to do nothing, keeping test output clean.

symbols.does: Sends a JSON HTTP response with proper content-length.
symbols.description: Utility method used by the mock supervisor to serialize an object, set headers, and write the response body.

symbols.does: Handles /sessions and /watch GET requests for the mock supervisor.
symbols.description: Returns the session state table on /sessions and, on /watch, replays the session's history first then streams live events from a queue until closed or timeout.

symbols.does: Handles /inject POST requests for the mock supervisor.
symbols.description: Records the injected body, then enqueues an injected marker event followed by a done event containing a synthetic supervised reply.

symbols.does: Writes a fake supervised registration file into the temp directory.
symbols.description: Creates a JSON registration file with transport=supervised, a supervisor session id, a supervisor base URL, and other fields so the channel layer can dispatch via the supervisor.
